name: Port
what_it_does: Internal developer portal platform with a REST API for building software catalogs, scorecards, and self-service actions across engineering teams.
use_cases: Query a live service catalog to find ownership, dependencies, and metadata for any service
Trigger self-service actions such as provisioning environments or creating repositories
Automate scorecard evaluation to measure and enforce engineering standards
Ingest data from CI/CD, cloud providers, and monitoring tools into a unified catalog
not_for: Teams without existing infrastructure to catalog (no value without data)
Simple project management without engineering workflow needs
best_when: Best when platform engineering teams need a unified catalog and self-service layer over heterogeneous infrastructure.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need a lightweight ticketing system rather than a full developer portal.

auth.notes: Client ID and Client Secret are exchanged for a JWT bearer token. Tokens expire and must be refreshed. Fine-grained role-based access control is available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Up to 15 users, unlimited blueprints and entities
pricing.paid_tiers: Paid plans from $8/user/month with advanced SSO, audit logs, and enterprise features
pricing.notes: Free tier is generous for small teams. Pricing scales per user on paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply per API key; contact Port for specifics on higher-volume plans

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns structured JSON errors with status codes and messages; MCP server error surface is narrower.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Entity upsert operations are idempotent by identifier; action triggers are not.
known_agent_gotchas: JWT tokens expire and must be refreshed before making calls; agents must handle token lifecycle
Blueprint schema must exist before entities can be ingested; agents need to handle schema-not-found errors
Self-service action execution is async; agents must poll for completion status

security_score_components.security_notes: JWT-based auth with expiring tokens is solid. RBAC available on paid plans. SOC 2 compliant.
